aliases: Tempeh and Tofu in Chilli Sauce

english: Tempeh and Tofu in Chilli Sauce

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general tempeh food product class; no specific entry for tempeh-tofu sambal belado combination dish. Closest parent match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP covers tempeh as a food category. No specific code exists for the combined tempeh-tofu sambal belado dish; closest parent match used for the primary ingredient.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for tempeh-tofu sambal belado dish. SNOMED CT coverage for prepared Southeast Asian soy dishes is limited.

serving.description: 1 standard serving (mixed tempeh and tofu with sambal)
serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of tempeh tofu sambal belado in a Singapore hawker or home setting is approximately 150g, comprising a mix of fried tempeh pieces and fresh tofu cubes coated in house-made sambal belado sauce.

nutrient_source.title: Tempeh Tofu — Singapore HPB Food Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Tempeh Tofu (cr_id G00010536), which reflects the prepared dish including sambal belado sauce. All per_100g values are taken from this canonical HPB record with confidence 0.95.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al.; analogous estimate based on mixed soy-based dish with moderate carbohydrate from sauce components
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for tempeh tofu sambal belado as a combined dish. GI estimated at 55 (low) based on the University of Sydney GI Database analogous entries: plain tofu GI ~15 (very low), tempeh GI ~41 (low), and the sambal sauce contribution of refined carbohydrate from added starch/sugar raising the blended dish GI toward the upper end of the low range. The high carbohydrate content (16g/100g) relative to the low fibre (0.2g/100g) supports a low GI estimate for the prepared dish, as 55 is still considered low GI. GL per 150g serving = (55 × 16.0 × 1.5) / 100 = 13.2.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium at 35mg/100g; calcium 118mg/100g supports vasodilation
hypertension.guidance: Sodium is well below the 200mg/100g threshold at 35mg/100g, making this dish suitable for hypertensive patients; however, patients should verify that preparation uses minimal soy sauce, as hawker-style additions can significantly increase sodium content.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Carbohydrate 16g/100g with very low dietary fibre 0.2g/100g indicating rapid glucose absorption from sauce components
type2_diabetes.guidance: The low fibre-to-carbohydrate ratio (0.2g fibre per 16g carbohydrate) suggests rapid glucose absorption; patients with type 2 diabetes should consume moderate portions (100–120g), pair with non-starchy vegetables, and request preparation with reduced sauce to lower the glycaemic impact.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low total fat 1.8g/100g, low saturated fat ~0.3g/100g, zero cholesterol, and fermented soy components with cardioprotective isoflavones
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The very low saturated fat content (~0.3g/100g), zero cholesterol, and presence of soy isoflavones from tempeh and tofu make this dish suitable for cardiovascular disease patients when prepared with minimal added oil and soy sauce.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Fermented tempeh supports gut microbiome and immune regulation; calcium 118mg/100g supports bone protection
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Tempeh's fermentation-derived probiotics support immune regulation relevant to RA management, and the calcium content (118mg/100g) provides bone protection for patients on corticosteroids; preparation should avoid deep-frying and excessive soy sauce.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Tempeh Tofu with Sambal Belado is a Malay and Indonesian-influenced soy-based dish commonly served in Singapore hawker centres and home kitchens. It combines tempeh — a firm, nutty-flavoured fermented soybean cake with a dense, chewy texture — and fresh tofu (bean curd), which is soft and mild, both cooked in a house-made sambal belado sauce. Sambal belado is a Minangkabau-origin chilli sauce made from red chillies, shallots, garlic, tomato, and a small amount of oil, producing a spicy, slightly tangy, and aromatic coating. The dish is typically served as a side dish alongside steamed rice, making it a protein and calcium source in a balanced Malay meal. Nutritionally, per 100g it provides 94 kcal, 3.5g protein, 1.8g fat, 16g carbohydrate, 118mg calcium, and only 35mg sodium. The relatively high carbohydrate content compared to the very low dietary fibre (0.2g/100g) reflects the sauce contribution and suggests moderate glycaemic impact. The dish is suitable for hypertensive and cardiovascular patients due to its low sodium and saturated fat profile. For type 2 diabetes management, portion control and reduced sauce are recommended. Tempeh's fermentation process provides probiotic benefit and supports gut microbiome health.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo canonical database entry for this exact dish (cr_id G00010536) with a recorded confidence of 0.95. The record is marked as reviewed and provenance is canonical. All core macros and secondary nutrients are present and internally consistent.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions, micronutrient vitamins, phosphorus, zinc, selenium, and moisture data for this prepared tempeh-tofu sambal belado dish are not available in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry as of 2026-04-16. Saturated fat estimated at 0.3g/100g based on the low total fat of 1.8g/100g and the predominantly plant-based fat profile of tempeh and tofu; this is consistent with USDA FoodData Central data for tempeh (SR Legacy item 16113) which reports ~0.7g saturated fat per 100g for plain tempeh, adjusted downward for the dilution effect of tofu and sauce in this combined dish. Cholesterol is 0.0mg/100g — confirmed zero per HPB source; this food is entirely plant-based with no animal-derived cholesterol. GI estimated at 55 (low) as an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database entries for tofu and tempeh, adjusted upward for the sauce-derived carbohydrate contribution. The nutrient profile shows an atypically high carbohydrate (16g/100g) and very low fibre (0.2g/100g) ratio compared to plain tempeh (~9g carb, ~9g fibre per 100g), consistent with the HPB description of a prepared dish with sambal sauce containing added starch or sugar. This dish is classified as Malay/Indonesian cuisine and is a Singaporean dish; full multilingual naming (English, Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) has been applied per G6 rules.
